audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Judas' remorse, Pilate, Barabbas, crowd dynamics, crucifixion, Psalm lament, death, veil tearing, tombs, centurion confession, women witnesses, burial, guard at tomb.

verses.restored: When morning came, all the chief priests and elders of the people took counsel against Jesus to put him to death.
Binding him, they led him away and handed him over to Pilate the governor.
Then Judas, the one handing him over, seeing that he was condemned, regretted it and returned the thirty silver pieces to the chief priests and elders,
saying, I sinned by handing over innocent blood. They said, What is that to us? You will see to it.
Throwing the silver into the temple, he withdrew; and going away, he hanged himself.
The chief priests took the silver pieces and said, It is not lawful to put them into the treasury, since it is blood-price.
Taking counsel, they bought with them the potter's field as a burial place for strangers.
Therefore that field has been called Field of Blood to this day.
Then what was spoken through Jeremiah the prophet was fulfilled: They took the thirty silver pieces, the price of the priced one, whom some from the sons of Israel priced,
and they gave them for the potter's field, as the Lord directed me.
Jesus stood before the governor, and the governor questioned him, saying, Are you the king of the Judeans? Jesus said, You say it.
While he was accused by the chief priests and elders, he answered nothing.
Then Pilate said to him, Do you not hear how many things they testify against you?
He did not answer him, not even one word, so that the governor marveled greatly.
At the feast the governor was accustomed to release one prisoner to the crowd, whomever they wanted.
They had then a notorious prisoner called Barabbas.
When they had gathered, Pilate said to them, Whom do you want me to release for you: Barabbas, or Jesus called Messiah?
For he knew they had handed him over because of envy.
While he sat on the judgment seat, his wife sent to him, saying, Have nothing to do with that righteous one, for today I suffered much in a dream because of him.
The chief priests and elders persuaded the crowds to ask for Barabbas and destroy Jesus.
The governor answered, Which of the two do you want me to release for you? They said, Barabbas.
Pilate said to them, What then shall I do with Jesus called Messiah? They all said, Let him be crucified.
He said, Why, what harm has he done? But they cried out all the more, Let him be crucified.
Pilate, seeing that he accomplished nothing but rather an uproar was beginning, took water and washed his hands before the crowd, saying, I am innocent of this blood; you will see to it.
All the people answered, His blood be on us and on our children.
Then he released Barabbas for them; but after flogging Jesus, he handed him over to be crucified.
Then the governor's soldiers took Jesus into the praetorium and gathered the whole cohort around him.
They stripped him and put a scarlet cloak around him.
Twisting a crown from thorns, they placed it on his head and a reed in his right hand; kneeling before him, they mocked him, saying, Hail, king of the Judeans.
Spitting on him, they took the reed and struck his head.
After they mocked him, they removed the cloak, put his garments on him, and led him away to crucify him.
Going out, they found a Cyrenian man named Simon; they compelled him to carry his cross.
Coming to a place called Golgotha, which means Place of a Skull,
they gave him wine mixed with gall to drink; tasting it, he did not wish to drink.
After crucifying him, they divided his garments by casting lots.
Sitting there, they kept watch over him.
Above his head they placed the written charge against him: This is Jesus, the King of the Judeans.
Then two robbers were crucified with him, one at the right and one at the left.
Those passing by blasphemed him, shaking their heads,
and saying, You who destroy the temple and build it in three days, save yourself. If you are Son of the Divine, come down from the cross.
Likewise also the chief priests, mocking with the scribes and elders, said,
He saved others; himself he cannot save. He is King of Israel; let him come down now from the cross, and we will trust in him.
He has trusted in the Divine; let the Divine rescue him now, if he desires him. For he said, I am Son of the Divine.
In the same way the robbers crucified with him also reproached him.
From the sixth hour darkness came over all the land until the ninth hour.
About the ninth hour Jesus cried out with a great voice, saying, Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani? That is, My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?
Some standing there heard and said, This one calls Elijah.
Immediately one of them ran, took a sponge, filled it with sour wine, put it on a reed, and gave him drink.
But the others said, Leave it; let us see whether Elijah comes to save him.
Jesus cried out again with a great voice and released the spirit.
And look, the veil of the temple was torn in two from top to bottom; the earth shook, and the rocks were split.
The tombs were opened, and many bodies of the sleeping holy ones were raised.
Coming out of the tombs after his rising, they entered the holy city and appeared to many.
The centurion and those with him keeping watch over Jesus, seeing the earthquake and what happened, feared greatly and said, Truly this was Son of the Divine.
Many women were there watching from afar, who had followed Jesus from Galilee, serving him.
Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James and Joseph, and the mother of the sons of Zebedee.
When evening came, a rich man from Arimathea named Joseph, who himself had become a disciple of Jesus, came.
He went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus. Then Pilate ordered it to be given.
Taking the body, Joseph wrapped it in a clean linen cloth
and placed it in his new tomb, which he had cut in the rock. Rolling a great stone to the door of the tomb, he went away.
Mary Magdalene was there, and the other Mary, sitting opposite the tomb.
The next day, after the Preparation, the chief priests and Pharisees gathered before Pilate,
saying, Lord, we remembered that while alive that deceiver said, After three days I am raised.

Therefore command the tomb to be secured until the third day, lest his disciples come, steal him, and say to the people, He was raised from the dead; and the last deception will be worse than the first.
Pilate said to them, You have a guard; go, make it as secure as you know how.
They went and secured the tomb, sealing the stone with the guard.
